summary:
SPIRITUAL EXAMINATION AND PERSONAL DISCIPLINE: Brandon shares his recent experiences of intense spiritual self-examination, feeling as though God is closely observing his life and habits. He recounts his ongoing struggle with weight and emotional eating, emphasizing the necessity of dietary discipline and daily self-monitoring. Brandon explains that believers must maintain a physically and spiritually bridled body to be prepared for the end times, viewing this discipline as a crucial aspect of spiritual readiness and obedience.

OVERCOMING OFFENSE AND CHURCH LEADERSHIP: Brandon discusses the widespread issue of fatherlessness within the church, noting that many young believers and leaders lack proper mentorship. Drawing from a book about the bait of Satan, Brandon reflects on his own past struggles with offense, which led him to abandon church attendance for a decade. He warns against becoming too familiar with pastors, emphasizing the need to maintain a respectful boundary between friendship and pastoral leadership to prevent toxic relationships and deep-seated bitterness.

GEOPOLITICAL PROPHECIES AND MILITARY THREATS: Brandon issues urgent warnings regarding global conflicts, specifically noting the fulfillment of a prior prophecy about Pakistan striking Afghanistan. He foresees imminent military action against Iran, predicting a regime change and the removal of its current leadership. Furthermore, Brandon warns of severe threats from Cuba, including hidden biological or chemical weapons linked to Russia and China. He urges intense prayer for the protection of American military forces, aircraft carriers, and bases in the Middle East against drone strikes carrying biological payloads.

POLITICAL CORRUPTION AND NATIONAL REVIVAL: Brandon predicts significant political shifts in the United States and abroad, foreseeing the exposure of deep-rooted corruption among politicians and cartel leaders. He specifically mentions the impending removal of a political figure in Minnesota and the collapse of major cartel operations in Mexico due to internal turf wars.
rawData: [February 27, 2026]-[The Lord showed us secret surprise payload drones coming against our troops]-[https://youtu.be/Ywv1xy-ko9U]-[Good evening, family. Thank you for joining us again today. I have an urgency to pray tonight, so I am not going to talk a lot. We are going to pray. We are just going to jump right in the river. I thought I would come prepared instead of having to cultivate it and then jump in the river. I have been doing it all day long for what he has had me on assignment for. Something major is about to happen, and as I stepped over in the spirit when I was listening to these songs, I have just been in that place of prayer all day. I have been in that place with Diana and having him go through and, like I said, I feel like I am being examined right now. You know, whenever you have his gaze—is what I want to call it—he is examining you. He is looking over you with that magnifying glass. I told her, I said, 'I feel like I am being stripped as you are asking him to go to the next level, as you are asking him to go to this next place.' She has been reading this book to me and going over all these things of the bait of Satan. This book is just beautiful and it is exciting. A lot of correction, a lot of going from glory to glory, changing you and making sure that you are examining yourself underneath that potter's wheel with him, saying, 'God, am I where I need to be?' I felt like we should just jump right in it. Don't worry about it. Okay. That is awesome. Sorry. I saw you eyeing me sometimes like, 'What?' I was like, 'What am I doing?' No, thanks, appreciate it. I was like, 'What am I doing? Is it my vitamins?' Well, no, it is fine. We do not have to do any of that right now. You were just making me nervous because you were eyeing my face and I was thinking, 'What did I do? What is wrong?' I like, 'You want to talk here? Take it, because you are making me nervous.' I know when you are staring at me, I am thinking, 'What is going on?' We are just real, y'all. But she is feeling better, you know. Yes. Thank you. Stupid COVID virus. Thank you all for your prayers. She says, 'I can taste things today.' Yes, I am so thankful. Thank you guys for praying for me. Eating lettuce without dressing, she goes, 'I don't even know why I'm eating.' She goes, 'If you can't taste dressing, why do you even eat it?' I started eating salads without any dressing on it. I couldn't taste because it has been about six days. It is just habits. Like, why am I doing this? I can't taste it. She cannot even taste it anyway. So why am I wasting calories on it? You know, going there, and that is where we are, too. I have been working hard on my eating right. One of the things the Lord has been talking to me about, like I said, is examining myself and examining our family and our eating habits and things that we are going over. I really started out the year thinking, 'Well, we are going to lose weight, we are going to exercise, and we are going to do all this stuff.' Then you let one month go by and then another one goes by, and here we are going on into the third month of the year this week. The Lord spoke to me in my dream again and I said, 'Okay.' So over the last four days, he got onto me. He said, 'How do you know where you are, Brandon, if you do not weigh every day? You must weigh every day. You must take account of everything you are putting in your mouth. Watch your calories, watch the things that you are doing.' It is not just, 'Well, you know, I am trying to eat right.' He said, 'You are eating right, but you are eating too much of eating right.' Watch and monitor the calories of what he restricted me to in a diet and logging it. Be intentional about your eating, so that you live this way. I thought about doing a weight loss challenge with everybody, because there are so many of the body of Christ that are overcoming this. Especially in the Pentecostal realm, the charismatics have a lot of weight issues. I have noticed. To be that fit body, to be that body that is ready for these end times, is going to be a body that is bridled. How can you be bridled if you are not bridled in your eating? That is why he was really like, I came on, you know, even with what I told you all about pressure and rejection and overcoming that bully mentality of what I went through as a kid, finding comfort in food, and going to the store and finding things that are organic. I am going to find this and it is organic, it is going to be okay. I will eat this ice cream or I will eat that because it is organic. And I don't know, it is just she is just teaching me. Going through the store today, I met one of our watchers, our Instagram watchers. Do I have stuff on my face still? No. Okay. Making sure. Well, I love you. You have any right to say something. But meeting somebody today in the grocery store and saying, 'Hey, I watch you on Instagram.' I thought, 'Wow, I forget that I am on Instagram,' because Braden is the one that does all that. I do not pay attention to all that social media stuff, but he is awesome. He uploads all that every day, all day doing that in the school. He is like, 'Dad, I put that out for you.' She was like, 'I watch you on Instagram.' I thought, 'Isn't that interesting? You don't watch us on YouTube. You watch us on Instagram.' Just going over things, y'all. I think it is just time to get real with stuff and really search ourselves out for what the Lord said about 1 Corinthians 5:5 this year. He said, 'I will hide you in the cliff, Brandon, for what is coming. I am going to shake the church. I am going to shake the nations, and it will be on the just and the unjust. There is going to be a major shaking to awaken in all these areas.' He said, 'I will hide my remnant in the cliff of the rock.' I saw that shaking, because if you are already awake and you are already dealing with stuff, he is not going to have to shake you to awake you. You are already awake. But I saw the body of Christ; there was a remnant of us in the cliff of this rock. I saw this hatch over it and the glory was over it. I just want to stay in that, like what Kathryn Kuhlman always says, 'Let me be the apple of his eye.' Where is that scripture about the apple of his eye, love? She talks about it all the time. I know it is in Psalms. David said it, I believe. But just asking him to search me and know me. I expect over the next 6 months that I am going to be 60 pounds lighter and I am going to go back to where I was whenever the Lord challenged me the first time in my sleep. Psalm 17:8, David prays, 'Keep me as the apple of the eye. Hide me under the shadow of thy wings.' Psalms what? 17:8. Keep me as the apple of eye. I want that. That your gaze is always upon me, that you are always looking towards me, that you are examining me. Even whenever there is something that you do not like, change it. Create in me that clean heart that whenever I stand before you, I am that vessel that has been purified by fire. Purify my heart. Let me be as gold. That ladle of his hand scooping the impurities out of your life, even whenever they are rising to the surface and you feel so vulnerable. That is what I have told her. I feel you feel so vulnerable lately when his gaze is upon you, when he is cleaning you up, and you are getting that self out of the way, and you are going, What hides? Well, your walk is an issue. Okay, what else can I do to exercise, take care of yourself, there are other things. Look at this. As you deal with that stuff, and when John read that little bit about what he said about leadership and about the fatherless, what I have always gone about the fatherless, the body of Christ that does not have a dad, there are a lot of fatherless children out there that need this. I felt like a lot of times, this part of this chapter spoke so loud to me. So I thought if it spoke so loud to me, maybe it would help you because there are certain things that she said today. Go ahead and just read some of that. It is really good. I have seen this cry. So, it says, Where are the fathers? I have seen this cry in countless men and women in the body of Christ. Most of them are young and with a strong call of God on their lives. They cry out for a father, a man to disciple, love, support, and encourage them. This is why God said that he would turn the hearts of the fathers, the leaders to the children, the people, and the hearts of the children to their fathers, lest I come and strike the earth with a curse. Malachi 4:6. Our nation lost its fathers, dads, leaders, or ministers in the 1940s and 1950s, and today our condition is getting worse. Not unlike Saul, many leaders in our homes, corporations, and churches are more concerned with their goals than with their offspring. Because of this attitude, these leaders view God's people as resources to serve their vision instead of seeing the vision as the vehicle to serve the people. The success of the vision justifies the cost of wounded lives and shattered people. Justice, mercy, integrity, and love are compromised for success. Decisions are based on money, numbers, and results. This opens the door to treatment such as David received. After all, Saul had a kingdom to protect. This type of treatment is acceptable in the leaders' minds because they are pursuing the furtherance of the gospel. How many leaders have cut off men under them because of suspicion? Why are those leaders suspicious? Because they are not serving God. They are serving a vision. Like Saul, they are insecure in their calling and that breeds jealousy and pride. They recognize qualities in people that they know are godly and they are willing to use those people as long as it benefits them. Saul enjoyed the success of David until he saw it as a threat to him. Then he demoted David and watched for a reason to destroy him. I have talked through with countless young men and women who cried out for accountability. They wanted to be submitted to a leader who would disciple them. They felt isolated and alone. They were seeking someone to father them. But God allowed them to go through rejection because God wanted to do in them what he had done in David. Listen carefully to what the Spirit is saying. David was concerned that Saul believed he was rebellious and evil. David must have searched his heart saying, Where have I gone wrong? How was Saul's heart turned against me so quickly? That is why he cried out, Someone urged me to kill you, but I said no. I only cut off the corner of your robe so that you could know and see that there is neither evil nor rebellion in my hand. See 1st Samuel 24. David thought if he could prove his love for Saul, Saul would restore him to favor and the prophecy would be fulfilled. People who have been rejected by a father or leader tend to take all the blame on themselves. They are imprisoned by tormenting thoughts of, What did I do? and, Was my heart impure? They sometimes wonder who turned the heart of my leader against me. Then they constantly try to prove their innocence to their leaders. They think that if they can only show their loyalty and value, they will be accepted. Sadly, the more they try, the more rejected they feel. So, I have dealt with this a lot. That paragraph right there is a summary of my life. I felt like when she read that to me, I was just going over this book, and it has helped me so much—the bait of Satan, and walking in offense towards leadership. Who I always talk about, you know, when I say, Where are the fathers? Where are the fathers of the faith? There is so much rejection, and in our case, the Lord has blessed us so much with so many people following us so quickly. It seems to be, the bigger it gets, the more rejection you receive from people. She brought up some other ministries. She said, Brandon, isn't it amazing? Have you ever seen so and so do a conference? They are always alone. Why? Because they have some of the biggest churches in Oklahoma. He has the biggest church in Oklahoma. He has plants around the whole world and he does not hang around this. And then she went over, do you know this pastor? And so we went over and I said, Isn't that interesting? They are criticized more than they are about what they are not doing or how they are not preaching right or whatever. And there is so much criticism towards it, but it comes out of a jealousy and an envy. People compare because they feel less than. And so I have realized as watching this, that is why some of these—because I always said, Why do they? When she brought that up today, she was talking to me about it. I said, Why do they do that? That is interesting, love. And we started going over it, and the Holy Spirit was ministering to me all day long. And then he was dealing with me with this kind of stuff. But we were praying for what is about to happen with Iran and the attacks on our soil, the storm that I feel is about to happen, that we are right at it. And when I went into the spirit, I have been very watchful over how I release things when I am seeing it even live. I hold things back a lot because of not feeling a release necessarily, because people cannot handle it, because you have such a variety of mature believers and immature believers. And so it is interesting because that is why I try to say, and maybe I come across harsh saying, there is 99.9% of you that have no idea how to have a relationship with him. And the ones that are mature, because the mature believers go, Well, that is offensive. And they are doing exactly what I am talking about. Don't be offended. I am not trying to pick at you. I am saying I have a lot of people that do not have a clue anything about the spirit realm and you are trying to educate because there are no fathers teaching it at church. There is not a lot of spiritual backbone at church anymore. And I have noticed when I have a survey and I ask people, Where do you go to church? We do not go to church. We watch online. We are offended. We don't want to go to church. And I come across more like to the lady today. She went to church, a local church in Tulsa. And it is very seldom that I come across somebody that says, Well, I go to church. So, well, we do not go to church. Well, why? Well, because we were offended. And that is why I wanted to bring this to the body of Christ even more after the Lord dealt with me about it. There are so many fatherless children. There are so many people that are not in church anymore because they do not want to go to church. They are offended at the pastor. They're offended at the Christian. You know, brother, I don't need church anymore. I find it online. I teach. And I was one of those for 10 years. Diana and I wouldn't go anywhere because of Megan, and we said, Well, the last time the usher told us to get up and leave because she was disrupting service, I vowed I'd never step foot back in another church again until she was better. Because it was so humiliating already to come because we couldn't put her in children's church because of how bad she was when she was really overcoming. And when the usher asked us, say, She's a disruption in the service, sir. Can you please leave? And I vowed at that time, I told her, I said, I will never go back to church, and we didn't for 10 years. We watched online only. And I'm just being really transparent. I'm just being real. And I said, You know what? Church isn't for us. We'll just watch TV. And I got so bitter and so offended in my heart about everybody in the body of Christ. And I let that wall of offense build a mountain until the Holy Spirit started stripping it down and I realized I was wrong. And yeah, that usher was rude, but he was just a cranky old man. I forgive him. And you move on. But I know there's a lot of you that walk in that offense. Well, you know, I was dealt this way. I was dealt that way. Why? Because I come across you all the time and people tell me I only watch online. I don't we are the church, brother. We don't need to go to church. But as I have read what Jesus said in Matthew 24 that many will be offended in these last days, there will be many false prophets. And you start to put it together that the ones that are not being shepherded, the ones that are off in la-la land over here by themselves out of offense, that spirit of offense, that root of offense is what makes it where you don't have discerning. You're not hearing him because if you can't forgive your brother of his trespasses, the Father said he won't forgive you of yours. So, you've allowed that offense and that root of bitterness to come in your heart so much that I don't want to hear anybody at church. I'm not going to go around the flock anymore. And we did it. I'm telling you. And it took a breaking down, and I started going, and we went to Billy Burke meetings, and that's when we started getting free. And I'm telling you, after you go to these things and you get a touch from the Father and you get a drink, you go, Man, I could just go and be a part of congregation. And I'm telling you, Glorious and these some other places, I don't like to mention it because I don't want everybody saying, Hey, we're going to move there. I want you to follow the voice of God for yourself. Don't go to church somewhere just because I go to church there. I want you to go to the church where God has called you to go to church, you know? So, I stopped mentioning a lot of places that I'm at all the time because people try to move from all over and they move here. One man moved from Florida, was here for a couple weeks, said, You know, Brian, this isn't for me. I've got to move back home. And I thought, Wow, that was sad. He moved over 2,000 miles, was here for a month, sold his house, sold everything to come where we were, and didn't like where we were because it wasn't for him, because he wanted to copycat everything what we're doing and moved back. I don't know if he was offended or not or disappointed or whatever, but moved his family back to the Orlando area, and it was amazing because I thought I got to watch really carefully what I say because there's so much influence in this channel that people will just pick up their stuff. One man moved from Chicago. These people, you just look at them going, Did God tell you to do that? Because they act miserable. What am I supposed to do now? Well, I don't know. I'm not your Holy Ghost. If he told you to do something, you're going to have instruction and you're going to know what he's told you to do. And that's what he went in this book. Diana was reading this book and going over things. So, just we're picking it like you would pick a chicken off a bone because I want to understand. I don't want to live in that. And so, with all the stuff that I overcome, y'all, we're all a work in progress. And I say it over and over and over again, but it's like when you're dealing with different levels, different devils, and you're dealing with all this stuff, you realize the pattern of offense will keep you out of the will of God. It will keep you in that place of lukewarm. And because you're always just so touchy, and you go from one place to another offended because of what they said to you, and you go to the next church and you're upset at them, and well, you know, they're just like the other people. And he talked about putting people at a standard. He goes, well, the world doesn't treat you that way. And I say, I used to say that all the time. I bet if I hung around Brad Pitt, he wouldn't be that way. Just being sarcastic. And you keep Why? Because what did John say in that book? He said it's because the standard that you keep the people. It's your expectation of them. What are you expecting of the body of Christ? You have them at a higher standard of what they should be than what the world would be right here. Go ahead. I realized that the love I was giving was being sown to the spirit and eventually I would reap those seeds of love. I didn't know from where, but I knew the harvest would come. No longer did I see it as a failure when love wasn't returned from the person I was giving it to. It freed me to love that person even more. If more Christians recognize this, they wouldn't give up and become offended. Usually, this is not the type of love we walk in. We walk in a selfish love that is easily disappointed when our expectations are not met. If I have expectations about certain persons, those people can let me down. They will disappoint me to the degree that they fall short of my expectations. But if I have no expectations about someone, anything given is a blessing and not something owed. We set ourselves up for offense when we require certain behaviors from those with whom we have relationships. The more we expect, the greater the potential offense. Yep. The greater the relationship, the greater the expectation, the greater the offense. And when you get around leadership, and that's what the Lord told me. He said even in this one, I don't want you to be friends with the pastors. I want you to honor them as pastors. They're not your friends. They're your pastors. There's so many people say, Well, if I need to go out to dinner with you, I want to know you and I want to hang out with you, let's fist bump and have a donut. But the more you get to know them, the more you realize that they're just as human as you are. And that's why I even told the people that we go, Hey, listen. I don't want to be your friend because you're my pastor. I want to separate the friendship from a pastor because the more you become my friend, it's harder for you to be my pastor. And you become too familiar with them and then you dishonor them. And that's the same thing with my friend Billy Burke. If you talk too much to them, you start looking at them as just a normal human being. But God put them in your life to be that iron sharpens iron and that mentor. There's a line that you don't cross even with joking with people. The Lord said always maintain an atmosphere of honor when you're talking to him. Do not dishonor him even if you feel well. I am just kidding around with you. That is why there is a place of honor that I try to keep; not that I am exalting a man of God, but I want to put that pastor in the place of, 'You are my pastor, you are not my friend.' Does that make sense? There are so many times that I see the congregation—even when I worked for that mega-church in Oklahoma City—always wanting to be the friend of the pastor. Let us go to lunch. Let us go have a golf game. I want to go with you to golf. Let us catch a movie or go to the gym and work out. They tried to find some kind of ground on how to relate. All of a sudden, that pastor they were teaching became no longer able to minister to them because they became too familiar with him and it becomes a toxic relationship very quickly. I am going through all the things that I have overcome. I have learned to pray and be an armor bearer, and the Lord said, 'Even where we are, sit on the back row and do what I have called you to do. It is not for you to have a platform here; it is for you to get refreshed and go dump out more. You have a platform; use it and minister to the millions. Your job is to get refreshed, drink, and have some substance so that you are refreshed and you walk away going, I can go on another day.' Because of the bombardment of growing and doing what God has called us to do, it is very interesting as you try to keep that equilibrium. Is that a good way to say it? It is an equilibrium. There are so many of you I know—because I have the emails and the conversations in public—that say, 'Hey, I am offended. I do not go to church anymore.' Well, where did the offense come in? How did that happen? 'Well, they said this to me.' Did you get too familiar with them? Were you a friend to the pastor? Did you allow them to correct you? Because I am telling you, iron sharpens iron. When you see those sparks fly, it happens. If you are not being challenged to grow... Diana said, 'Brandon, the books that make me grow the most, like these ones, are the ones that I love.' They are the ones that challenge you, where you have to think about it for a little bit longer. You said, 'I want to throw it out the window when I am driving.' Yeah, they make you get mad and you go, 'That is trash, I do not want to do that anymore.' But it is sharpening, it is challenging, and it makes you self-examine. Judge not lest you be judged; you judge yourself and think, 'Where does this fit me and where am I going wrong?' There is somebody who said they read this book four times a year because it keeps them on track from being offended. He said he reads it at the first of every quarter. I thought, 'That is a wow.' But it is how I am with the things the Lord has told me, even with deflecting and giving Him the glory. I realize that I am like an airplane that just got off course. Just a little bit. The more you stay off course, the more you are flying way over here. The Lord said, 'Brandon, I told you to do this, this, and this,' and I go, 'Oh, forgive me.' He will instruct you about your giving, where He wants you tithing, and where He wants you to stay on course. There is so much—if you do not have those refreshers—there are distractions that pull you off. It just takes a little bit; just a little nudge gets you off course. It is the same thing with offense in your marriage and all kinds of things. I try to bring these things to you because it really helps me. It is the small foxes that ruin the vines, not the big ones. Most people are looking for the major decisions: the divorce, or whatever you are dealing with. It did not start with a divorce or adultery; it started with something small. It was the small thing that you did not correct while it was small that exploded into something way over here. You ask, 'How did we get this far off course?' It is because you did not correct it back there. Go back to what He instructed you to do before you even got to that place. There are the footsteps to keep you on course so that you do not deviate. If you do not correct it right there, the whole thing is falling down. That is why I am telling you today, let that magnifying glass examine you. I feel like I have His gaze; He is observing me. There is this need to be fixed immediately. I stopped. How did I get so fat again? I am just being real. I weighed today and I was 287 lbs. I said, 'Where did I go wrong?' It was not the one ice cream; it was the snacking that added up day after day. If you do not catch it, the habits end up making you big. Habits form patterns. It is something so simple and innocent at the time, but it is the little thing that takes you off course. When you are way over here, you go, 'Oh Lord, I did not mean to.' Stress, financial pressure, life pressure, daughter pressure; you allow that thing to push you and you have unhealthy habits. You weigh yourself and you go, 'The clothes that are the biggest are getting tight. Correct it.' I am here again. Same mountain, same thing. You go around that mountain all the time. You did not learn from the last time. I told her before, when I was sacrificing and was exercising so hard, riding five miles a day on my bike. I'll never be here again. I'll never do this again. The next thing you know, one bite at a time, you go over here. That's why I challenge myself today. By the time my birthday comes, I want to be a certain amount of weight again, but I don't want it to be some place I visit. I told her, I said, How do you renew your mind to it that this is not a place you visit? That the Holy Spirit says this is a place you must live, for I want you here and I have a job for you to do. If you keep going down this road, Jack Coe went home early because of obesity and he had a major call on his life and he was warned. Jack Coe went home early; there are other big ministers that went home early that had major calls on their lives that died early because they had addictions to things. Some people had alcohol addictions. Some people went off in weird left-field theology. Some people went off and did certain things. You go, I'm not going to throw rocks at glass. You know, what do you say? Braden says, Well, you have a speck in your own eye and all that. We go over that all the time, but I've just tried to be somebody that you all know because I know there's so many people that deal with certain things. Does that make sense with y'all tonight? I mean, it all started with offense; that is where a lot of it comes back to the root, is offended at stuff, offended at comments, offended at people. You try to bury things with food and the bully mentality, coping mechanisms. Some people grab alcohol, some people grab marijuana, some people grab maybe some drugs or whatever. Some people grab lust and pornography or bad material to read or whatever, but certain people deal with certain things. I'm not sitting here justifying anything. I'm just trying to say the only one that is going to ever set us free—you'll know the truth and the truth will set you free. But when you start to realize, hey, I've got a problem, it's realizing it and confronting it and embracing it and saying, Listen, I'm going to expose this. I don't want to live this way no more. I don't want to be a statistic. I don't know, it's just healthy things. Where are we going to be with the body of Christ with this end-time glory, going from glory to glory? We all have something. We've all got something we're working through. But I believe the root of many things starts with offense, or bitterness towards your wife, your husband, your kids, your pastor, your boss, your neighbor, your mom, or your dad. You've got something that you didn't get rid of the root of, and you thought, I've got to fix this. So Braden told me, I never try to sell you anything. Never. Never will I ever try to sell you. I try very hard to always do exactly what the Holy Spirit has done, and I've done it. He showed me this thing about these little emojis. YouTube will let us do little emojis on the chat. When you all want to talk, you can use custom ones that are a dollar a piece. You can charge whatever you want for them. I would do it completely for free. The minimum is 99 cents. That's the lowest you can go. I can put these little stickers that you all can use, like, go tubing, the greater glory things that are fun that Braden wanted to design. I never want to get you all upset about anything because it's a dollar. I would give it away for free, but it's YouTube's thing. I think it's fun and I told him, if you want to do it, he's told me, Dad, are the people going to be offended because it's a dollar? I said, I don't know, but I'll present it to them because it's him. He's wanting to do it and he thinks it's fun to make these little emojis because he watches the chat with you all.
